key_points: Gopi was a kind and imaginative girl who received a magical brush from a mysterious man
The brush had the power to bring drawings to life, but it had to be used only to help the poor
Gopi used the brush responsibly to help villagers by painting useful objects
The greedy Zamindar tried to misuse the magic brush for wealth and power
Gopi’s creativity and courage helped her escape from the Zamindar and continue helping others
examples: Gopi painted a bowl of khichdi which became real and helped feed hungry people
She painted a plough and shawl to help villagers with their daily needs
The Zamindar demanded that Gopi paint treasures and a fortress
Gopi painted a winding road and a horse to escape from prison
She painted a wide river to stop the Zamindar and his soldiers from chasing her
formulas_or_processes: Gift → Gopi receives the magic brush from the mysterious man
Purpose → The brush must be used only for helping the poor
Conflict → The greedy Zamindar demands wealth from Gopi
Solution → Gopi uses creativity to escape and protect the magic brush
Outcome → Kindness and imagination defeat greed
exam_tips: Understand the symbolic meaning of the magic brush as creativity and responsibility
Remember the contrast between Gopi’s kindness and the Zamindar’s greed
Focus on how Gopi’s imagination helps solve problems in the story
Explain the moral clearly: power and talent should be used to help others
